en:
The next Olympic Games will be held in our country in four years.
A new stadium will be built near the capital, and more roads will be added.
An athletes' village is being designed to international standards.
Huge swimming pools and training halls will be completed before the Games.
Millions of visitors will be welcomed by volunteers from every city.
By the opening day, the whole nation will be ready for this immense event.
cn:
下一届奥运会将在四年后在我国举行。
首都附近将建新体育场，还会修建更多道路。
一座符合国际标准的运动员村正在设计中。
大型泳池和训练馆将在赛前全部完工。
数百万游客将由来自各城市的志愿者接待。
到开幕那天，全国都将为这场盛会做好准备。

en:
My friend Harrison had lived in the south for many sunny years.
After he retired, he decided to settle in England again.
He bought a lovely house and thought he had planned everything well.
Soon it rained continually, and the rooms became cold and damp.
He complained bitterly because he missed the sunshine abroad.
In the end he sold the house and left, having forgotten only the weather.
cn:
我的朋友哈里森在南方阳光地带住了很多年。
退休后，他决定再次回英国定居。
他买了一栋漂亮房子，以为自己已计划周全。
不久雨就下个不停，房间变得又冷又潮。
他苦苦抱怨，因为他想念国外的阳光。
最后他卖掉房子离开了，因为他唯一忘记考虑的就是天气。

en:
Mr Gilbert had just had an operation and was lying in bed.
He felt anxious, so he telephoned his doctor at home.
He did not give his name and asked whether the patient was all right.
The doctor recognized his voice and replied that everything was fine.
Mr Gilbert asked if the operation had been successful.
At last the doctor smiled and said that he would recover very soon.
cn:
吉尔伯特先生刚做完手术，正躺在床上。
他很焦虑，于是给医生家里打电话。
他没有报姓名，只问那位病人是否安好。
医生听出了他的声音，回答说一切顺利。
吉尔伯特又问手术是否成功。
最后医生笑着说他很快就会康复。

en:
At dinner the hostess seated me next to Mrs Rumbold.
I tried to start a conversation, but she remained completely silent.
She looked only at the dishes and ate every course quickly.
I asked polite questions about weather and theatre, yet she gave no reply.
While the other guests were talking, she was busy with food.
At the end she finally spoke and asked for one more dessert.
cn:
晚宴上，女主人把我安排在伦博尔德太太旁边。
我试着打开话题，但她始终沉默。
她只盯着菜肴，每道菜都吃得很快。
我礼貌地问天气和戏剧，她却没有任何回应。
其他客人在聊天时，她一直忙着吃东西。
直到最后她才开口，说要再来一份甜点。

en:
My wife tried on hats in the shop while I waited impatiently.
She asked the assistant for one hat after another.
At last I said, "Do you call that a hat?" in a rude tone.
She turned and asked whether I remembered my own shopping last year.
I said every hat was ridiculous, but she kept smiling.
In the end I paid the bill and carried the new hat home.
cn:
我妻子在店里试帽子时，我在一旁不耐烦地等着。
她一顶接一顶地让店员拿帽子。
最后我语气刻薄地说：“你把那个也叫帽子吗？”
她转过身问我是否记得我去年买帽子的样子。
我说每顶都很可笑，但她一直微笑。
最后我还是付了账，把新帽子拎回了家。

en:
At a village market, a snake charmer sat beside a basket.
As soon as he began to play his pipe, the snake rose slowly.
People gathered round and threw coins while he repeated the same tune.
Many believed the snake loved music and danced to the melody.
In fact snakes cannot hear, and the animal followed the moving pipe.
The crowd left amazed, though the player was not very musical.
cn:
在乡村集市上，一位耍蛇人坐在篮子旁。
他笛子一吹，蛇就慢慢立了起来。
他反复吹同一支曲子时，人们围拢并投下硬币。
很多人都以为蛇爱音乐，会跟着旋律起舞。
其实蛇听不见声音，它只是跟着晃动的笛子。
人群惊奇地散去，尽管吹笛人并不太懂音乐。

en:
In 1929 Admiral Byrd planned a flight over the South Pole.
Before takeoff, his team had placed supplies along the route.
Soon after they started, the plane faced high mountains and heavy weight.
They had to drop sacks and extra fuel to climb higher.
Only then could the aircraft pass safely through the gap.
After many dangers, they succeeded in flying over the Pole and returning.
cn:
1929年，伯德上将计划飞越南极。
起飞前，他的队伍已经沿途放好了补给。
出发不久，飞机就遇到高山和超重问题。
他们不得不扔下袋子和多余燃料来爬升。
只有这样，飞机才能安全越过山口。
经历重重危险后，他们成功飞越极点并返航。

en:
Mrs Sterling was taking her children through a forest for a picnic.
While they were walking along a narrow path, two men rushed out.
They snatched her handbag and ran towards the river.
She left the children safely and chased the thieves at once.
As soon as they reached the water's edge, she caught one by the strap.
After a hard struggle, she got her bag back and saved its contents.
cn:
斯特林太太正带孩子穿过森林去野餐。
当他们沿着狭窄小路前行时，两名男子突然冲出。
他们抢走她的手提包，朝河边跑去。
她把孩子安顿好，立刻追赶劫匪。
他们刚到河边，她就抓住了包带。
经过激烈拉扯，她夺回了包，也保住了里面的东西。

en:
Sam lost his wallet on the way to town and worried for days.
Villagers said it had been stolen with all his savings.
A week later the wallet was returned by post in an envelope.
No name was given, but every note was still inside.
Before it came back, Sam had almost lost hope.
He counted the money publicly and thanked the unknown honest person.
cn:
萨姆进城途中丢了钱包，担心了好几天。
村民们都说钱包连同积蓄一起被偷了。
一周后，钱包被人装在信封里邮寄了回来。
没有署名，但每一张钞票都还在。
在钱包回来前，萨姆几乎已经绝望。
他当众点清了钱，并感谢那位匿名诚实人。

en:
A cargo plane landed in London and boxes were unloaded quickly.
One heavy crate was opened because strange sounds were heard inside.
A young man was discovered hiding among the goods.
He had hidden there before takeoff to avoid paying the fare.
The trip was extremely uncomfortable, and he looked half frozen.
Instead of saving money, he was told to pay a huge fine.
cn:
一架货机降落在伦敦，货箱很快被卸下。
其中一只沉重木箱因里面传来怪声而被打开。
人们发现一名年轻人藏在货物中。
他在起飞前就躲进箱子里，想逃票。
这段旅程极其难受，他看起来几乎冻僵。
结果他不但没省钱，还被要求交一大笔罚款。

en:
An old pub that was said to be haunted has just been bought.
The new landlord is brave and has offered free whisky to any ghost.
Since this joke was announced, curious people have visited every night.
The furniture has stayed quiet, and no spirit has appeared yet.
Even so, business has improved because everyone wants to see the place.
A thirsty ghost has not come, but many thirsty customers have.
cn:
一家据说闹鬼的老酒馆刚刚被买下。
新老板很大胆，还放话给任何幽灵免费威士忌。
自从这个玩笑传开后，每晚都有好奇的人来。
家具一直很安静，至今没有鬼现身。
即便如此，生意反而变好了，因为人人都想来看。
嗜酒的幽灵没来，但口渴的顾客来了很多。

en:
I went to the dentist to have one bad tooth extracted.
Each time I tried to speak, he gently pushed me back into the chair.
He finished quickly and then asked whether I had wanted to tell him something.
I replied that I had wanted to speak from the beginning.
I said he had pulled out the wrong tooth by mistake.
The dentist stared at me in silence, and I could only smile sadly.
cn:
我去看牙医，想拔掉一颗坏牙。
每次我想开口，他都轻轻把我按回椅子。
他很快做完后问我，刚才是否想说什么。
我回答说我从一开始就想说话。
我告诉他，他不小心拔错了牙。
牙医沉默地看着我，我只能苦笑。
